judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with substantively equivalent findings; Report 1 is slightly more precise/actionable (explicit removal calls for the legacy secret and explicit corrected tfvars line for the OIDC fix), while Report 2 leaves the legacy-secret fix as a replacement rather than removal and states the OIDC fix implicitly rather than explicitly.

report_1.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true" ... Change: delete the line. ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" ... Change: delete the line; this account should not exist in prod at all. ... Change: remove SHADOW_SCORING_SINK entirely from prod ... LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ... Change: delete the line; don't recreate a secret injection for a consumer that no longer exists.

report_2.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true" — a debug toolbar ... must not be on in production. Should be "false". ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" — a QA email is hardcoded as a superadmin account ... Should be removed entirely (empty) ... SHADOW_SCORING_SINK ... Either the sink must be emptied, or if shadow scoring is genuinely wanted in prod, it needs its own prod-scoped sink and the enabled flag must actually gate the write path.

report_1.evidence: Net effect: the frontend bundle shipped to production would have https://staging-api.example.com baked into it as VITE_API_BASE_URL, because it was built once with --mode staging and never rebuilt. This is precisely the "rebuild per environment; don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap ... that file needs to be created (with the real production API URL) before a --mode production build would even pick up a value.

report_2.evidence: So the production deployment ships a frontend bundle whose API base URL is hardcoded to https://staging-api.example.com. If applied as-is, the production frontend would call the staging API, not a production API, unless something downstream (not present in this repo) rewrites it. ... there's no .env.production and no --mode production build step, so a prod-specific bundle is never produced by this pipeline as written.

report_1.evidence: No migration step anywhere in deploy.yml. The runbook (docs/prod-runbook.md:6) states "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in deploy.yml runs alembic upgrade or equivalent ... don't trust "the pipeline succeeded" as proof the prod schema is current; this needs to be confirmed/added explicitly, and it directly contradicts the (3-month-old, likely stale) runbook.

report_2.evidence: No database migration step. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no step in deploy.yml runs alembic or any migration tool ... so even if a runner existed, the prod schema would not actually be created by them.

infra/prod/main.tf:1-4 instantiates the same module but with oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — unchanged from staging, i.e., it was copied verbatim and never edited to say environment:production. ... Net effect: as committed, production cannot authenticate distinctly from staging.
